tier_justification: Single work-unit (gate/bashPatterns.ts's matchesBashSendPattern) with no publish/deploy/migration/data-deletion/external-send surface of its own — it's the classifier that gates those actions, not one of them — so tier-2's outward/irreversible predicate does not fire. Not claimed tier-0 despite an existing test file already covering much of the goal state: this is a security-relevant gate (a false negative here means an unapproved send executes unblocked) and these evals were authored post-implementation (frozen_at is real authoring time, disclosed per instruction, not backdated) rather than pre-build, so treating it as standard tier-1 rather than self-exempting keeps an independent P0 floor in place instead of resting solely on the PR's own test suite.

amendments.why: DISCLOSED GAP: this evals.json was authored after implementation was complete (PR #70 already had 5 RCA-item-14 holes closed, then 3 additional gaps found and fixed during review), not frozen before build as rule 1 requires. frozen_at is the real authoring timestamp, not backdated. The task came from an RCA fix list with no pre-frozen pr-scope evals.json, per orchestrator instruction. Oracle independence (rule 4) is still honoured: assertions below are derived from the goal state (RCA item 14's description + the send-gate's own stated purpose in gate/sendGate.ts) and probe the compiled regex behaviour directly via node, not by importing or re-running gate/bashPatterns.test.ts.

evals.assert: A curl calendar-events write using any of curl's documented body-inference flags (--data*, -d, --json, --form/-F, including short flags bundled behind another short flag like -sd/-sF) is classified as a send requiring approval, without relying on an explicit -X POST marker.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && node -e "const {matchesBashSendPattern}=require('./gate/bashPatterns.ts'); " 2>/dev/null; npx tsx -e "import { matchesBashSendPattern } from '/Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50/gate/bashPatterns.ts'; const cmds=[\"curl https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events --data '{}'\", \"curl https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events --json '{}'\", \"curl https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events -F summary=x\", \"curl -sd '{}' https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events\", \"curl -sF summary=x https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events\"]; const results=cmds.map(c=>matchesBashSendPattern(c)); if(results.every(r=>r===true)){console.log('ALL_TRUE');process.exit(0);}else{console.log('FAIL',JSON.stringify(results));process.exit(1);}"
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && npx tsx -e "import { matchesBashSendPattern } from '/Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50/gate/bashPatterns.ts'; const r = matchesBashSendPattern('curl https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events'); if(r===false){console.log('CONTROL_CORRECTLY_FALSE_BUT_FAILING_BY_DESIGN');process.exit(1);}else{process.exit(0);}"
evals.evidence: cmd exited 0 printing ALL_TRUE (verified via npx tsx run 2026-07-24). negative_control exits 1 by construction — it demonstrates matchesBashSendPattern returns false for a plain GET on the same path, proving the check is not a tautology (a broken always-true implementation would make this control impossible to fail, since it would also return true for the GET).

evals.assert: Read-only calls on the same route surfaces the write patterns target (Slack chat.getPermalink, Telegram getUpdates, Gmail drafts list, calendar GET with -D/--dump-header) are NOT classified as sends — the gate must not over-block routine reads into approval friction.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && npx tsx -e "import { matchesBashSendPattern } from '/Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50/gate/bashPatterns.ts'; const cmds=['curl https://slack.com/api/chat.getPermalink?channel=C1&message_ts=1','curl https://api.telegram.org/bot123:ABC/getUpdates','curl https://www.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/drafts','curl -D headers.txt https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events','curl --dump-header /dev/null https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events']; const results=cmds.map(c=>matchesBashSendPattern(c)); if(results.every(r=>r===false)){console.log('ALL_FALSE');process.exit(0);}else{console.log('FAIL',JSON.stringify(results));process.exit(1);}"
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && npx tsx -e "import { matchesBashSendPattern } from '/Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50/gate/bashPatterns.ts'; const r = matchesBashSendPattern('curl -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage -d \\'{}\\''); if(r===true){process.exit(1);}else{process.exit(0);}"

fixtures.formula: n/a — negative_control below is a direct assertion inversion, not a piped formula; fixtures omitted deliberately

smoke.negative_control_output: (exits 1 because chat.postMessage IS correctly classified true, inverting the assertion to prove the control fails for a real reason)

evals.evidence: cmd exited 0 printing ALL_FALSE (verified via npx tsx run 2026-07-24) — all 5 negative-surface reads correctly unclassified as sends. negative_control deliberately asserts the wrong thing (that a known send is NOT a send) and fails, proving the check isn't vacuously true.

evals.assert: The full project test suite and typecheck pass at the PR's head SHA — no regression introduced anywhere in the repo by this change.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && npm run typecheck && echo TYPECHECK_OK
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/assistant-agent/.claude/worktrees/agent-abf9d1a2d1915ff50 && npx tsc --noEmit --strict false /dev/null/nonexistent-file-for-negative-control.ts; test $? -ne 0 && echo CONTROL_FAILS_AS_EXPECTED && exit 1 || exit 0
evals.evidence: npm run typecheck exits 0 cleanly (verified 2026-07-24 at SHA 6603527). negative_control points tsc at a nonexistent file, which correctly fails, proving the tsc invocation itself is capable of a non-zero exit.
